Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add automatic iCloud Drive backup (#504)

## Summary

- Add automatic backup system that writes encrypted PDF backups to a
user-selected iCloud Drive folder whenever vault data changes
- Introduce `BackupStorageProvider` protocol for pluggable backup
destinations, with an iCloud Drive implementation using security-scoped
bookmarks
- Auto-backup triggers on vault mutations (items, tags, imports) with a
5-second debounce, and skips backup if payload hash is unchanged
- Configurable retention policy (7 days, 30 days, 1 year, forever) with
automatic cleanup of old backups
- New `AutoBackupSettingsView` integrated into the backup screen with
status display, provider selection, retention picker, and error handling

## Test plan

- [x] 28 unit tests for `AutoBackupServiceImpl` covering configuration,
status transitions, provider selection, cleanup, publishers, and error
paths
- [ ] Manual: enable auto-backup, select iCloud Drive folder, verify
backup appears after editing a vault item
- [ ] Manual: verify old backups are cleaned up per retention setting
- [ ] Manual: verify error states display correctly (no folder selected,
iCloud unavailable, no backup password)
- [ ] Manual: verify backup skips when no data has changed

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add pass-3 feature engineering: ml_dataset_features_20260310_v2.csv (831 rows × 103 cols, 35 feats) (v3.14)

- add_high_value_predictors.py: 23 new feat_* columns across 4 groups
  - Company dependency / existential risk (6): n_trials, n_unique_drugs,
    single_asset_flag, lead_asset_dependency_score, n_late_stage_trials,
    pipeline_concentration_simple
  - Regulatory state (6): orphan, fast_track, breakthrough, nda_bla,
    priority_review flags + regulatory_stage_score (0–3 ordinal)
  - Trial quality (6): enrollment_log, randomized, controlled, withdrawn,
    terminated flags + trial_quality_score (extends pass1 design_quality)
  - Reaction priors (5): mean |ATR-move| by phase, therapeutic superclass,
    phase×superclass cross, market_cap_bucket, market_cap_bucket prior
- ml_feature_dict_20260310_v2.csv: 38 entries, unified schema (stage, feature_type,
  transformation_logic, coverage, missing_count)
- Superseded v1 dataset and dict archived; ml_dataset_mesh_final.csv removed

Move all scene setup into SceneDelegate directly

Create SKView and present MenuScene directly in SceneDelegate.scene()
instead of relying on GameViewController. This eliminates any view
lifecycle timing issues by setting up everything at window creation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add protocol erosion detection, ghost session prevention, minimum handoff requirements

New sections from 60+ session validation:
- Principle finding #7: protocol discipline is perishable
- Phase 1: ghost session detection step, anti-pattern for skimming docs
- Phase 1.5: Claim the Session (write stub before work begins)
- Phase 6: minimum handoff requirements table (6 mandatory items),
  evidence rules (no "need to verify", no fabrication)
- Protocol Erosion section: erosion pattern, why it happens,
  6 detection warning signs, 4 prevention measures
- Honest Accounting: fabrication as terminal failure mode
- Quality Gates: expanded 10 → 12 (session claimed, handoff meets minimum bar)
- Performance Tracking: erosion indicators
- Session template: ghost session check, handoff minimum fields

Evidence: Sessions 62-63+ demonstrated that 14 consecutive clean
deliveries can degrade to 1/10 scores within 12 hours when sessions
stop re-internalizing the protocol.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(output): route pino logs and human text to stderr, keep stdout clean (#10)

Pino logger was writing to fd 1 (stdout), mixing structured JSON logs
with command output. Human text output from commands also went to
stdout via console.log. This broke piping and JSON parsing.

Changes:
- Logger: pino.destination(1) → pino.destination(2) (stderr)
- validate: human text output uses console.error instead of console.log
- list: human file listing uses console.error instead of console.log
- render: human YAML output uses console.error instead of console.log
- JSON output (--output json) remains on stdout for all commands
- Updated all command tests to assert on capturedErrors for human output

stdout is now reserved exclusively for machine-readable output.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Bs1o4y1edSLjbqj6UtCjew

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

[Slurm] Add canonical GPU name resolution for GRES scheduling (#8901)

* [Slurm] Add canonical GPU name resolution for GRES scheduling

Canonical GPU names like `H100` now resolve to raw GRES types like
`nvidia_h100_80gb_hbm3` at scheduling time, using vendor-prefix
stripping and prefix matching (similar to Kubernetes).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [Slurm] Add raw→canonical GPU name display + shared CANONICAL_GPU_NAMES

Move CANONICAL_GPU_NAMES to sky/utils/gpu_names.py so both K8s and Slurm
share the same list. Add canonicalize_raw_gpu_name() for the display path
so sky show-gpus shows clean names (e.g. H100-80GB instead of
NVIDIA_H100_80GB_HBM3).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [Slurm] Use segment subsequence matching for GPU name resolution

Replace prefix check + hardcoded -80GB special case with general
segment subsequence matching. Handles all memory variants
(A100-SXM4-80GB, V100-PCIE-32GB, etc.) in both display and
scheduling paths. Add V100-32GB to CANONICAL_GPU_NAMES.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [Slurm] Simplify resolve_gres_gpu_type + restore K8s GPU names import

* [Slurm] Use resolve_gres_gpu_type in check_instance_fits + cleanups

- check_instance_fits now calls resolve_gres_gpu_type so it checks
  CPU/memory against the same nodes that will actually be scheduled,
  eliminating a consistency gap between fitness check and deploy time.
- make_deploy_resources_variables gracefully falls back to the
  user-provided GPU name if resolution fails.
- Rename segs -> segments for clarity.
- Add inline comments to the sort key in resolve_gres_gpu_type.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* improve unit tests

* [Slurm/K8s] Import CANONICAL_GPU_NAMES from gpu_names directly

Remove re-export from kubernetes/constants.py — all callers now
import from sky.utils.gpu_names directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [Slurm] Add inline comments to _is_segment_subsequence

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: v4 with archiving, update detection, retry, sync history, and bug fixes

- Auto-archive master doc when it exceeds configurable size threshold
- Detect and re-sync meeting notes modified after first sync
- API retry with exponential backoff (500ms → 1s → 2s)
- Sync history stored in ScriptProperties, viewable from menu
- Manual archive menu item (forceArchive)
- Fix Drive query syntax: appProperties has { key=... } instead of appProperties/key
- Fix file export: UrlFetchApp + OAuth token instead of Drive.Files.export()
- Fix archive clear on large docs: lightweight endIndex fetch via fields mask
- Better participant extraction (multi-line, email cleanup)
- More aggressive Gemini text cleaning (markdown, excess whitespace)
- Timezone from Session.getTimeZone() with Europe/Paris fallback
- Updated READMEs to reflect actual v4 behavior and config

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(d2c): correct SDTM filename, upload dir, and ack dir per FinCEN specs

Per SDTMFilenameExamples.pdf from FinCEN Help Desk (ticket #00491214):
- File type prefix: FFBARST → FBARXST (valid: SARXST/CTRXST/DEOPXST/FBARXST)
- Upload directory: "submissions/" → root ("do NOT specify a landing spot")
- Ack directory: "acks/" → "Inbox"
- Ack extensions: .ACK → .ACKED (and .MESSAGES.XML)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Story 3.2 — entity & relationship extraction via local LLM

Two-phase Ollama extraction pipeline per chunk: entity extraction
(Person/Organization/Location) followed by relationship extraction
(WORKS_FOR/KNOWS/LOCATED_AT) when 2+ entities found.

- EntityExtractionService with MERGE deduplication in Neo4j by
  (name, type, investigation_id); confidence_score updated to max
- Sync Neo4j driver for Celery tasks (mirrors sync_postgres pattern)
- Neo4j uniqueness constraints and investigation_id indexes created
  at FastAPI startup via ensure_neo4j_constraints
- extracting_entities pipeline stage with entity.discovered SSE events
  fired per unique entity per document run
- document.complete now includes entity_count and relationship_count
- Soft failure per chunk for LLM parse errors; Neo4j errors fail document
- 180 tests, 0 regressions (43 new tests across 3 files)

Code review fixes: sync_neo4j_driver closed at shutdown, weak test
assertions corrected, migrations/env.py documented in File List.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix event card styling — remove double borders, use dividers

Replace individual card borders with simple bottom dividers to
eliminate the double-border look with the widget container.

- lesson-22: esteem bar now tracks cursor via mousemove/touch; label overlap fixed
- lesson-24: h1 now includes Materialism title word

- lesson-31: grade tracker and week planner made mobile-scrollable
- lesson-41: opening activity grid min-width reduced for mobile
- lesson-43: talent/skill grids collapse to single column on mobile
- lesson-47: removed orphaned btn-container outside scroll-container
- lessons 23,34,35,36,37,38,39,40: migrated from old inline CSS to lessons-common.css

- catalogue.html: vivid neon gradient background; 12 distinct month header colours

Tighten event card spacing

Reduce gaps between cards, smaller font sizes, better meta alignment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix blank screen: use SKView as subview with viewDidAppear

Stop overriding loadView. Instead add SKView as a subview in viewDidLoad
and present the scene in viewDidAppear when bounds are guaranteed valid.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: skip scope validation in OAuth callback for Workspace accounts

Google Workspace may return fewer scopes than requested (e.g. omits
calendar.readonly when calendar.events is granted). Passing
check_response_scopes=False prevents the mismatch error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: harden PNG metadata extraction against untrusted input

- Validate zTXt compression method byte (must be 0 per PNG spec)
- Limit zlib decompression output size to prevent memory exhaustion
- Add size guard on downloaded image data before parsing metadata
- Add tests for invalid compression method, oversized decompression,
  and download size validation

commit.message:
fix: stop level validation skipped active sides when stopped side had 0

Full IC with one stopped side had put_side_stop=0, which triggered
SAFETY check and skipped the ENTIRE entry including the active call
side. Now validates stop levels per-side, only for non-done sides.

commit.message:
Migrate generation history from Clerk privateMetadata to Upstash Redis

Clerk metadata has a ~4KB size limit which caused 422 errors.
Redis has no practical size limit and supports native list operations
(lpush, ltrim, lrange, lrem) for efficient history management.
Max history restored to 50 entries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Display pit locations on Team@Event and Event Teams pages (#1207)

Fetch pit location data from the TBA API team status endpoints and
display it in two places:

- Team@Event Summary tab: shown in a new "Info" section with a link to
  the FRC Nexus pit map, crediting FRC Nexus in the label
- Event Teams tab: shown as a "Pit X" prefix in each team's subtitle

Data is fetched as MutableStateFlow (no Room persistence) so it's
always fresh when the user opens the page.

commit.message:
Fix black screen and compiler warnings

- Move scene presentation to viewDidLayoutSubviews so the SKView has
  its correct bounds before creating the scene (fixes black screen)
- Remove unused lastX variable in generateInitialStairs
- Remove unused idx binding in update loop

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix empty ~/.hermes dir bypassing Hermes install check

Typing 'hermes' in bash creates an empty ~/.hermes/ directory, which
was detected as a valid Hermes install. Now checks for actual Hermes
files (config.yaml, .env, package.json, hermes.js) before considering
the directory valid.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add connection lines, murdoku puzzles, per-puzzle leaderboard, UI improvements

- 5 new murdoku puzzle templates (5x5 to 16x16) with themed regions and gen-murdoku.cjs script
- Per-puzzle time leaderboard pinned at bottom of left panel
- InfoPanel split into fixed top (title/timer/buttons), scrollable middle (rules/clues), fixed bottom (leaderboard)
- In-progress puzzles shown on localhost with construction-tape border styling
- Puzzle list: completion counter, difficulty labels, capitalized tags, Expert difficulty
- Fix: fog labels with revealWithFog hidden until group revealed even on unfogged cells
- Fix: color-only solutions (cells:{}) now validate correctly
- Fix: inProgress flag preserved in index by both build-index.js and vite plugin
